05 February 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Lethal Kinetic Strike, Feb. 5, 2026 SOUTHCOM 05 Feb 2026 -- On Feb. 5, at the direction of #SOUTHCOM Commander Gen. Francis L.Donovan, Joint Task Force Southern Spear conducted a lethal kinetic strike on a vessel operated by Designated Terrorist Organizations.

  • U.S. Coast Guard marks 200,000 pounds of cocaine seized in Operation Pacific Viper US Coast Guard 05 Feb 2026 -- The U.S. Coast Guard announced Thursday it has seized more than 200,000 pounds of cocaine in the Eastern Pacific Ocean since launching Operation Pacific Viper in early August.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Meeks, Smith, Himes Statement on the Expiration of the New START Treaty House Armed Services Committee - Democrats 05 Feb 2026
  • A shared burden: African Lion 26 planning concludes, uniting NATO and African partners for regional security Army News 05 Feb 2026 -- Allies representing Tunisia, the United States, France and Italy met in Tunis Jan. 21-24 for the final planning event of the Tunisian portion of African Lion 2026.
  • USS Delbert D. Black Departs Israel Following Port Visit CENTCOM 05 Feb 2026 -- Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer USS Delbert D. Black (DDG 119) departed Eilat, Israel, following a scheduled port visit, Feb. 1.
  • U.S. Navy and Royal Saudi Naval Forces Conclude Exercise Indigo Defender CENTCOM 05 Feb 2026 -- U.S. Naval Forces Central Command (NAVCENT) and the Royal Saudi Naval Forces West Fleet (RSNF-WF) concluded the NAVCENT-led, bilateral exercise Indigo Defender 2026, Feb. 5.
  • U.S., Lithuania Strengthen NATO Eastern Flank Through Integrated HIMARS Live Fire DoD News 05 Feb 2026 -- In the snow-covered woods of Lithuania, the sound of an M142 High Mobility Artillery Rocket System pierced through the sky, sending a clear message of readiness and resolve on NATO's eastern flank.
  • Shaping the future of airpower: Air Force requirements transformation industry day US Air Force 05 Feb 2026 -- The United States Air Force hosted industry partners Jan. 29, 2026, as part of its effort to transform the Requirements Process under the Secretary of War's mandates that each service overhaul the requirements and acquisition systems.
  • The U.S. and Russian Federation agreed to reestablish high level military-to-military dialogue EUCOM 05 Feb 2026 -- Following the productive and constructive progress towards President Trump's goal of peace in Ukraine made by Jared Kushner and Special Envoy Steve Witkoff over the past week, the U.S. and Russian Federation agreed today in Abu Dhabi to reestablish high level military-to-military dialogue.

  • US Congress Approves Funds For Baltics As Russia Tests NATO's Eastern Flank RFE/RL 05 Feb 2026 -- The US Congress has approved $200 million in security assistance for the Baltic states as Russian activity along NATO's eastern flank intensifies.
  • 'Most visible' NATO exercise in 2026, STEADFAST DART, begins NATO Allied Land Command 05 Feb 2026 -- Allied Command Operations (ACO) officially launched NATO exercise Steadfast Dart 2026 across Central Europe, Jan. 15.

  • New START's end raises alarm over return of US-Russia nuclear rivalry Press TV 05 Feb 2026 -- The impending expiration of the New START, the last remaining nuclear arms control treaty between the United States and Russia, has heightened fears that decades of fragile restraint are giving way to a more dangerous, unregulated nuclear era.
  • Russia Open to New START Talks, But Only If US Responds Constructively - Kremlin Sputnik 05 Feb 2026 -- Russia will conduct dialogue with the United States on the New START Treaty if Washington gives constructive answers, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Thursday.
  • New START Expires, Russia Assesses It Negatively - Kremlin Sputnik 05 Feb 2026 -- The New START (New Strategic Arms Reduction Treaty) expires, Russia assesses this negatively and expresses regret in this regard, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Thursday.
  • Russia to Analyze Behavior of Other Nuclear-Armed Nations After New START - Intel Chief Sputnik 05 Feb 2026 -- Russia will behave responsibly and analyze the behavior of other countries with nuclear weapons after the New START (New Strategic Arms Reduction Treaty) expires, Russian Foreign Intelligence Service (SVR) Director Sergei Naryshkin said on Thursday.
  • China expresses regret over expiration of New START Treaty between Russia and the US: FM spokesperson Global Times 05 Feb 2026 -- China expresses regret that the New START Treaty between Russia and the US has expired, which has had great significance for maintaining global strategic stability, the Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said on Thursday.
  • Russian Space Troops successfully launch Soyuz-2.1b missile carrier from Plesetsk Cosmodrome Russia MoD 05 Feb 2026 -- On 5 February, at 21:59, the Russian State Test Cosmodrome (Plesetsk Cosmodrome) successfully launched a Soyuz-2.1b medium-class carrier rocket with spacecraft in the interests of the Russian Ministry of Defence.

  • Finland to send another package of defence materiel to Ukraine Finnish MoD 05 Feb 2026 -- Finland will deliver its 32nd materiel assistance package to Ukraine. The President of the Republic decided on the matter on 5 February 2026 on the proposal of the Government.
  • Defence Forces expand their arsenal with new Baton Optik drones capable of destroying enemy equipment beyond 30 km Ukraine MOD 05 Feb 2026
  • Starlink terminals on the whitelist remain operational, while russian terminals have already been blocked Ukraine MOD 05 Feb 2026
  • Defence Ministry initiates regulation of the medical support system for foreign volunteer fighters Ukraine MOD 05 Feb 2026
  • 30,000 enemy military personnel eliminated and record Shahed UAV shootdowns: Unmanned Systems Forces results for January Ukraine MOD 05 Feb 2026
  • Ukraine brings home 157 service members and civilians freed from russian captivity Ukraine MOD 05 Feb 2026
  • Government approves updated roster of Deputy Ministers at the Ministry of Defence of Ukraine Ukraine MOD 05 Feb 2026
  • In January, more than 1,000 hectares of de-occupied areas were cleared by demining units of the Ministry of Defence of Ukraine Ukraine MOD 05 Feb 2026
  • How to complete Starlink verification: The Ministry of Defence answers the most frequently asked questions Ukraine MOD 05 Feb 2026
  • AFP warns vs. 'too good' jobs after OFW's reported capture in Ukraine Philippine News Agency 05 Feb 2026 -- The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) reminded all prospective Filipino overseas workers to be wary of accepting job offers that are too good to be true to avoid being deployed to fight in conflict zones.

  • PLA Navy commissions new Type 052D destroyer; Chinese military affairs expert highlights type's continuous production and upgrades Global Times 05 Feb 2026 -- The Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) Navy has commissioned yet another new Type 052D guided missile destroyer, the Ganzi(Hull Number 128), with its training footage released by official media on Thursday. A Chinese military affairs expert said that the Type 052D is a very capable class of warship that is suitable for mass production and continued upgrades, and ships of this class are making significant contributions to China's national defense.

  • Military police deploy tactical vehicles in Taipei key facility drill CNA 05 Feb 2026 -- Various types of wheeled infantry fighting vehicles (IFV) cruised through the streets of Taipei Thursday as the military police conducted a drill to protect key facilities in Taiwan's capital, the Military News Agency reported.
  • Taiwan's submarine prototype continues submerged tests CNA 05 Feb 2026 -- Taiwan's indigenous defense submarine (IDS), Hai Kun (海鯤), sailed from a shipyard in the southern port city of Kaohsiung on Thursday morning for its latest round of submerged tests, one week after a previous round was deemed successful.
  • U.S. House committee advances bill on space cooperation with Taiwan CNA 05 Feb 2026 -- A bill aimed at strengthening space cooperation between the United States and Taiwan cleared the committee stage in the U.S. House of Representatives on Wednesday.

  • Iran's deterrence power strengthened after ballistic missile upgrades: Top general Press TV 05 Feb 2026 -- Iran's top military official says the country has strengthened its deterrence by upgrading domestically manufactured ballistic missiles.

  • Defence Secretary visits Forward Areas in Bhuj - witnesses Jointness, Infrastructure Development and Capability Building Initiatives India PIB 05 Feb 2026 -- Defence Secretary Shri Rajesh Kumar Singh visited forward areas in the Creek Sector in Bhuj, Gujarat on 04-05 February 2026
  • 24th India-US Joint Technical Group Plenary held at DRDO HQs in New Delhi to advance cooperation in Defence Science & Technologies India PIB 05 Feb 2026 -- Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO) hosted the 24th Indo-US Joint Technical Group Plenary Meeting in New Delhi on February 03-04, 2026

  • His Excellency Shavkat Mirziyoyev, President of the Republic of Uzbekistan, along with senior ministers paid an official visit to Global Industrial & Defence Solutions (GIDS) as part of his visit to Pakistan. ISPR Pakistan 05 Feb 2026
  • Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir, NI (M), HJ, COAS & CDF, visited Muzaffarabad today. ISPR Pakistan 05 Feb 2026

  • Iraq - Contracted Logistical Services for VACIS XPL Passenger Vehicle Scanning Systems DSCA 05 Feb 2026 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Iraq of Contracted Logistical Services for VACIS XPL Passenger Vehicle Scanning Systems and related equipment for an estimated cost of $90 million. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress of this possible sale today.

  • Israel Ministry of Defense Signs Approx $130 Million Deal with Elbit Systems to Integrate Israeli Systems on CH-53K Helicopters Israel MOD 05 Feb 2026 -- As part of the preparations for receiving the CH-53K "Pereh" helicopters, the Defense Procurement Directorate (DPD) within the Israel Ministry of Defense (IMOD) has signed a deal with Elbit Systems to integrate advanced Israeli technologies, including command and control, avionics, and electronic warfare systems, and the advanced anti-missile DIRCM system, on the 12 new helicopters expected to replace the IDF's aging "Yas'ur" helicopters.

  • US Congress approves USD 200 million for Baltic military assistance Latvia MFA 05 Feb 2026 -- On 3 February 2026, the US Congress took an important decision for European and Baltic security by approving USD 200 million funding for the continuation of the Baltic Security Initiative in 2026, aimed at reinforcing the defence capabilities of the Baltic states and deepening defence cooperation with the United States.
  • Memorandum of Understanding signed for the establishment of an anti-tank mine production facility in Latvia Latvia MOD 05 Feb 2026 -- On Thursday, February 5, Minister of Defence Andris Sprūds participated in the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding regarding the establishment of a specialized production facility for anti-tank mines and their essential components in Latvia.
  • A. Sprūds: Baltic Unity and Ability to Act Are Our Main Responses to Security Challenges Latvia MOD 05 Feb 2026 -- From 29 to 30 January, Defence Minister Andris Sprūds participated in a meeting of the Baltic Defence Ministers Committee in Tallinn, Estonia.

  • Medical Personnel from NATO Countries Trained in Sub-Arctic Conditions Swedish Armed Forces 05 Feb 2026 -- Tasked with operating in sub-Arctic climates, medical personnel from six NATO countries took part in advanced medical training conducted by the Swedish Armed Forces Defence Medicine Centre.
  • Swedish Armed Forces Invests in space capabilities Swedish Armed Forces 05 Feb 2026 -- Starting in 2026, around ten Swedish satellites will enhance operational capability and strengthen both Sweden's and NATO's capacity in the space domain. Through new agreements signed by the Swedish Defence Materiel Administration (FMV), intelligence and surveillance resources from space will be delivered to the Swedish Armed Forces.

  • NATO Multinational Battlegroup has merged with the German Brigade Litauen stationed in Lithuania Lithuania MND 05 Feb 2026 -- February 4, the Lithuanian Armed Forces Infantry Brigade Iron Wolf transferred the colors of the NATO Multinational Battlegroup to the German 45th Armored Brigade Litauen at a ceremony at the Confluence Park in Kaunas.

  • Karen Brutyan received the Chief of the Defence Staff of the Indian Armed Forces Ministry of Defence of the Republic of Armenia 05 Feb 2026

  • 75 years on - Anzac relationship strengthens as strategic environment evolves New Zealand Defence Force 05 Feb 2026 -- For Colonel Lisa Kelliher, the New Zealand Defence Force's (NZDF) newly appointed Defence Adviser to Australia, Canberra represents both professional familiarity and the centre of New Zealand's most important defence partnership.

  • No shift in alliance as US focuses on Western Hemisphere - PH envoy Philippine News Agency 05 Feb 2026 -- There is no expected major change in the deep defense partnership between the Philippines and the United States as the latter increases focus on the Western Hemisphere, said Philippine Ambassador to the US Jose Manuel Romualdez.
  • DND exec hails Thai support for PH military, ASEAN 2026 Philippine News Agency 05 Feb 2026 -- A ranking Department of National Defense (DND) official has expressed the country's gratitude to Thailand for its continuing assistance in boosting the country's military capabilities and support for the Philippines' chairship of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) 2026.
  • Navy to set up monitoring station in Tubbataha Philippine News Agency 05 Feb 2026 -- The Philippine Navy (PN) on Thursday announced that its Western Naval Command (WNC), which is based in Puerto Princesa, Palawan, will establish a naval monitoring detachment (NMD) at the Tubbataha Reefs Natural Park Ranger Station to boost situational awareness in the Sulu Sea.

  • Spanish MFA closes world's largest debate on responsible use of military AI in A Coruña Spain MFA 05 Feb 2026 -- The MFA highlights Spain's leadership in promoting the use of artificial intelligence in the military sphere in favour of international peace and security

  • Chairman Rogers and Ranking Member Smith Led Bipartisan Delegation to India and Thailand House Armed Services Committee - Democrats 05 Feb 2026 -- House Armed Services Committee Chairman Mike Rogers (R-AL) and Ranking Member Adam Smith (D-WA), led a bipartisan congressional delegation to India and Thailand along with Rep. Salud Carbajal (D-CA) and Rep. Jimmy Patronis (R- FL).

  • Romanian Minister of Foreign Affairs Oana Țoiu positions Romania as a core transatlantic partner in economic security at the Washington rare minerals ministerial hosted by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and US Vice-president J.D Vance Romania - Ministry of Foreign Affairs 05 Feb 2026 -- As a strategic partner of the United States and a core EU member country in the rare minerals domain, Romania joined the first rare minerals ministerial at the invitation of Secretary of state Marco Rubio.

  • Joint Statement on the Inaugural Meeting of the Joint Steering Committee of the U.S.-DRC Strategic Partnership Agreement US Dept. of State 05 Feb 2026 -- The text of the following statement was released by the Governments of the United States of America and the Democratic Republic of the Congo.
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Bolivian Foreign Minister Aramayo US Dept. of State 05 Feb 2026
  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Mongolian Foreign Minister Battsetseg US Dept. of State 05 Feb 2026

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 5 February 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 05 Feb 2026
  • UN Police 'indispensable' to fulfilling peacekeeping mandates, Security Council hears UN News 05 Feb 2026 -- The head of UN Peacekeeping on Thursday called for greater support for international police officers deployed to field missions at a time when financial resources are dwindling and multilateralism is waning.
  • The meaningful participation of women in UN policing is an operational necessity: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 05 Feb 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador Archie Young, UK Deputy Permanent Representative to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on UN Peacekeeping Operations: Police Commissioners.

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Hamas slams latest Israeli strikes on Gaza, says Netanyahu aims to derail truce Press TV 05 Feb 2026 -- Hamas has condemned deadly Israeli airstrikes in Gaza, which killed at least 23 Palestinians in one of the deadliest days since October's ceasefire, saying the attack proves Israel's campaign of genocide is ongoing.
  • 21 Palestinians killed in Israeli attacks across Gaza Strip: civil defense People's Daily 05 Feb 2026 -- At least 21 Palestinians, including a paramedic, were killed in Israeli attacks across the Gaza Strip on Wednesday, the Palestinian Civil Defense in Gaza said.

  • Trump invites PH to join 'Board of Peace' -envoy Philippine News Agency 05 Feb 2026 -- United States President Donald Trump has invited the Philippines to join the Board of Peace (BOP), a US-initiated multinational body aimed at advancing post-conflict solutions, specifically the Gaza reconstruction.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Central African Republic, Chad, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Ukraine UNOCHA 05 Feb 2026

  • Security forces have successfully concluded Operation Radd-ul-Fitna-1 in Balochistan under which a series of coordinated, swift, and intelligence driven operations were conducted against Indian sponsored terrorist elements seeking to disrupt peace and development by targeting innocent civilians, including women and children. ISPR Pakistan 05 Feb 2026

  • Pakistan Reaffirms Solidarity with the Kashmiri People on 'Kashmir Solidarity Day' Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 05 Feb 2026 -- Today, the Government and the people of Pakistan are observing Kashmir Solidarity Day, reaffirming their steadfast commitment to stand shoulder to shoulder with their Kashmiri brothers and sisters in their just struggle for the realisation of their right to self-determination.
  • Message from the Deputy Prime Minister/Foreign Minister of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan on Kashmir Solidarity Day (5 February 2026) Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 05 Feb 2026 -- As we observe the Kashmir Solidarity Day today, Pakistan reiterates its firm and unwavering support for the people of Jammu and Kashmir in their just struggle for the right to self-determination. Under illegal Indian occupation, the Kashmiri people remain deprived of their fundamental rights. Yet, their resilience testifies the legitimacy of their just cause.
  • Message from the Prime Minister of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan on Kashmir Solidarity Day (5 February 2026) Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 05 Feb 2026 -- Every year, 5th February is observed as Kashmir Solidarity Day, an occasion where the people and the Government of Pakistan reaffirm their principled and unwavering support for their Kashmiri brothers and sisters in their just struggle for the realization of their right to self-determination.
  • On the solemn occasion of Kashmir Solidarity Day, Field Marshal Syed Asim Munir, NI (M), HJ, COAS & CDF; Admiral Naveed Ashraf, NI, NI (M), T Bt, Chief of the Naval Staff; and Air Chief Marshal Zaheer Ahmed Baber Sidhu, NI (M), HJ, Chief of Air Staff, on behalf of the Armed Forces of Pakistan, reaffirm their unwavering support for the resilient people of Indian Illegally Occupied Jammu and Kashmir (IIOJK) in their epic struggle for self-determination. ISPR Pakistan 05 Feb 2026

  • Child malnutrition hits catastrophic levels in parts of Sudan UN News 05 Feb 2026 -- Acute malnutrition among children has reached catastrophic levels in parts of Sudan's North Darfur and Greater Kordofan, UN-backed analysts warned on Thursday, as conflict, mass displacement and denials of aid push the country deeper into a famine-risk emergency.
  • UK imposes fresh sanctions to dismantle Sudan's 'war machine' UK FCDO 05 Feb 2026 -- The UK has imposed new sanctions against 6 individuals fuelling Sudan's war, following the Foreign Secretary's visit to the Sudan-Chad border.
  • Sudan: Foreign Secretary's statement, 5 February 2026 UK FCDO 05 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper gave a statement to Parliament following her trip to Adré on the Sudanese border in Chad.

  • M23 rebels: UN sees progress in talks but warns violence persists UN News 05 Feb 2026 -- The United Nations has welcomed signs of progress in talks aimed at reducing violence in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo.

  • Chad: Africa's refugee haven struggles with its own stark challenges UN News 05 Feb 2026 -- Amid the ongoing war in Sudan, Chad, the country receiving the most refugees in Central Africa, saw slight improvements in its humanitarian situation last year, but as one of the most vulnerable nations on the African continent, it is still struggling to support four million people in need.

News Reports

  • Indian gov't team reaches Kerala to assess bird flu situation People's Daily 05 Feb 2026 -- A federal government team has arrived in the southern Indian state of Kerala to assess the situation regarding avian influenza, commonly known as bird flu, officials said Wednesday.

  • Press release on summoning the head of the diplomatic mission of the Federal Republic of Germany to the Foreign Ministry Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 05 Feb 2026

  • Statement to the House of Representatives Prime Minister of Australia 05 Feb 2026

  • The OIC condemns terrorist attacks in central and northern Nigeria Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 05 Feb 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) has expressed its strong condemnation and denunciation of the terrorist attacks carried out by gunmen on Tuesday, 03 February 2026, on villages in central and northern Nigeria, which left dozens of innocent people dead and injured
  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on Nigeria UN Secretary-General 05 Feb 2026

  • Year of Unity of the Peoples of Russia opens President of Russia 05 Feb 2026 -- The President took part in a ceremony to launch the Year of Unity of the Peoples of Russia.
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's remarks at a presentation of the Perm Territory, Moscow, February 5, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 05 Feb 2026
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's interview with RT television channel, Moscow, February 5, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 05 Feb 2026
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's opening remarks at a meeting with President of the National Assembly of the Republic of Armenia Alen Simonyan, Moscow, February 5, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 05 Feb 2026
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's upcoming talks with OSCE Chairperson-in-Office, Federal Councillor of the Swiss Confederation Ignazio Cassis and OSCE Secretary General Feridun Sinirlioglu Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 05 Feb 2026
  • Estonia will close nighttime traffic at the southern road border crossings for three months starting 24 February Estonia MFA 05 Feb 2026 -- Due to border incidents originating from Russia, the government today made a preliminary decision to close nighttime traffic at the Southern Luhamaa and Koidula road border crossing points on the Estonian-Russian border for three months.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on February 5, 2026 PRC MofA 05 Feb 2026
  • China will firmly safeguard lawful rights, interests of its enterprises: FM on Panama ports ruling Global Times 05 Feb 2026 -- Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said on Thursday that China has repeatedly stated its position on issues related to relevant ports in Panama. The State Council Hong Kong and Macao Affairs Office recently published an article pointing out that the relevant ruling by Panama's Supreme Court disregards facts, violates the principle of good faith, and seriously undermines the legitimate rights and interests of China's Hong Kong enterprises. The article stressed that the Chinese government firmly safeguards the lawful and legitimate rights and interests of Chinese companies, Lin said.
  • China opposes 'small-circle' rules that undermine global trade order: FM on US-led 'critical minerals bloc' Global Times 05 Feb 2026 -- A Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson said on Thursday that maintaining an open, inclusive, and universally beneficial international trade environment serves the common interests of all countries, and that all parties have the responsibility to play a constructive role in safeguarding the stability and security of global supply and industrial chains for critical minerals.
  • Xi says ready to work with Trump to steer giant ship of China-U.S. ties steadily forward People's Daily 05 Feb 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping said on Wednesday that he hopes to work with U.S. President Donald Trump in the new year to steer the giant ship of China-U.S. relations steadily forward through wind and storms, and accomplish more big things and good things.
  • Xi says ready to work with Trump to steer giant ship of China-U.S. ties steadily forward PRC MND 05 Feb 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping said on Wednesday that he hopes to work with U.S. President Donald Trump in the new year to steer the giant ship of China-U.S. relations steadily forward through wind and storms, and accomplish more big things and good things.
  • Xi calls on China, Russia to grow ties, work for global strategic stability People's Daily 05 Feb 2026 -- China and Russia should increase high-level exchanges and strengthen pragmatic cooperation in various fields, Chinese President Xi Jinping said in a virtual meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin on Wednesday.
  • Hong Kong to set out its 1st five-year plan, says HKSAR chief executive People's Daily 05 Feb 2026 -- Hong Kong will draft its first five-year development blueprint as part of efforts to better align with the national 15th Five-Year Plan (2026-2030), John Lee, chief executive of China's Hong Kong Special Administrative Region (HKSAR), said on Wednesday.

  • Mainland urges Taiwan authorities to remove cross-Strait air transport restrictions People's Daily 05 Feb 2026 -- A Chinese mainland spokesperson on Thursday urged Taiwan authorities to remove unreasonable restrictions on the air transport across the Taiwan Strait.
  • Xi urges Trump to be prudent about arms sales to Taiwan in latest phone call CNA 05 Feb 2026 -- The leaders of the world's two superpowers discussed the Taiwan issue during a phone call on Wednesday, with Chinese President Xi Jinping (習近平) urging U.S. President Donald Trump to handle arms sales to Taipei "with prudence."
  • MAC slams '1992 Consensus' push after CCP, KMT top figures meet in Beijing CNA 05 Feb 2026 -- Taiwan's Mainland Affairs Council (MAC) denounced the "1992 Consensus" as a framework aimed at annexing Taiwan, following a political meeting between China's fourth-ranking official Wang Huning (王滬寧) and Kuomintang (KMT) Vice Chairman Hsiao Hsu-tsen (蕭旭岑) in Beijing on Wednesday.
  • Taiwan question at the core of China's core interests, says Taiwan Affairs Office spokesperson after Chinese, US leaders' phone talk Global Times 05 Feb 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping on Wednesday talked with US President Donald Trump over phone. President Xi emphasized that the Taiwan question is the most important issue in China-US relations. Taiwan is China's territory, and China must safeguard its own sovereignty and territorial integrity, and will never allow Taiwan to be separated, he said. The US must handle the issue of arms sales to Taiwan with prudence, Xi said, according to a readout from the Chinese foreign ministry.
  • Top political advisor meets Taiwan delegates to CPC, KMT think tank forum People's Daily 05 Feb 2026 -- Top political advisor Wang Huning on Wednesday met with the Taiwan delegation attending the think tank forum co-hosted on Tuesday by research institutes affiliated with the Communist Party of China (CPC) and the Chinese Kuomintang (KMT) party.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 05 Feb 2026

  • Spokesperson's Press Briefing (February 5, 2026) ROK MFA 05 Feb 2026

  • Iran's newly unveiled ground stations to boost satellite control, speed up image reception: ISA Press TV 05 Feb 2026 -- Head of the Iranian Space Agency (ISA) Hassan Salarieh has announced that the launch of the Salmas and Chenaran ground stations has enabled more precise control of Iranian satellites and faster reception of satellite imagery.
  • Fragile US-Iran Talks Salvaged For Oman Summit RFE/RL 05 Feb 2026 -- In a dramatic reversal that has kept global markets and regional allies on edge, the United States and Iran have confirmed they will move forward with high-stakes negotiations in Muscat on February 6.
  • Trump May Choose Military Option on Iran If Diplomacy Fails - Vance Sputnik 05 Feb 2026 -- US Vice President JD Vance said President Donald Trump will "keep his options open" on Iran, pursue "non-military means" first, and could ultimately use force.
  • Foreign Ministry: Iran FM heads to Oman for nuclear talks with US Press TV 05 Feb 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei says Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has departed for Oman at the head of a diplomatic delegation to hold nuclear talks with the US, aimed at achieving a just, honorable and mutually acceptable understanding that safeguards Iran's national interests.
  • Araghchi: Nuclear talks with US slated for Friday in Oman IRNA 05 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says that Iran-US talks on the nuclear issue are scheduled for Friday in Muscat, Oman.
  • Russia's UN envoy rejects military option against Iran, calls for diplomacy Press TV 05 Feb 2026 -- Russia's United Nations ambassador has strongly rejected any military options against Iran, warning such actions could destabilize the entire region, while stressing Moscow's readiness to support diplomacy without imposing itself as a mediator.
  • Iran seizes two vessels smuggling fuel in Persian Gulf IRNA 05 Feb 2026 -- Iran has seized two fuel-smuggling vessels near Farsi Island in the Persian Gulf, uncovering more than one million liters of smuggled fuel.
  • IRGC Navy seizes two fuel smuggling vessels in Persian Gulf Press TV 05 Feb 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy has announced the seizure of two fuel smuggling vessels near Farsi Island in the Persian Gulf.

  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: THIRD LAUNCH PAD (TLP) AT SATISH DHAWAN SPACE CENTRE India PIB 05 Feb 2026 -- The geo-technical investigation and topography survey of the site is completed in the third quarter of 2025 subsequent to the financial approval, obtained on March 21, 2025
  • Transcript of Weekly Media Briefing by the Official Spokesperson (February 05, 2026) India MEA 05 Feb 2026
  • Transcript of Special Briefing by Secretary (East) on the Prime Minister's visit to Malaysia (February 05, 2026) India MEA 05 Feb 2026
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: EXPANSION OF NUCLEAR POWER CAPACITY India PIB 05 Feb 2026 -- The present installed nuclear power capacity in the country is 8,780 MW comprising of 24 nuclear power plants (excluding RAPS-1 - 100 MW).
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: NUCLEAR ENERGY India PIB 05 Feb 2026 -- The Government has announced a Nuclear Energy Mission to reach a capacity of 100 GW nuclear power capacity by 2047
  • PARLIAMENT QUESTION: SCALING UP NUCLEAR ENERGY India PIB 05 Feb 2026 -- The safety and performance of nuclear power plants are continuously reviewed internally by NPCIL and periodically by the regulatory authority, AERB

  • Meeting of the Prime Minister with the President of Uzbekistan and Delegation Level Talks Pakistan - Prime Miinister's Office 05 Feb 2026

  • PM call with President Trump of the United States: 5 February 2026 Gov.UK 05 Feb 2026
  • New UK-US partnership to drive investment and bolster critical minerals supply chains UK FCDO 05 Feb 2026 -- The UK and US have signed a Memorandum of Understanding on critical minerals in Washington DC.
  • UK and US sign Memorandum of Understanding on critical minerals UK FCDO 05 Feb 2026 -- The UK and US signed a Memorandum of Understanding on critical minerals in Washington DC on 4 February.

  • Australia-Germany Foreign Ministers' Joint Statement Germany Federal Foreign Office 05 Feb 2026

  • Cuban Government Updates Its Transition to Martial Law Plan - President Sputnik 05 Feb 2026 -- The Cuban government has updated its plan for transitioning the country to martial law in the event of possible external aggression, Cuban President Miguel Diaz-Canel Bermudez said on Thursday.
  • Delivering Additional Humanitarian Support Directly to the Cuban People US Dept. of State 05 Feb 2026 -- Building on the success of the recently announced partnership with the Catholic Church and Caritas, the Trump Administration is announcing an additional $6 million in direct assistance for the Cuban people.
  • Cuba: UN warns of possible humanitarian 'collapse', as oil supplies dwindle UN News 05 Feb 2026 -- The UN on Wednesday warned of potential humanitarian "collapse" in Cuba, following Washington's attempt to block oil supplies from reaching the island.

  • State Minister for Foreign Affairs HORII's meeting with Under Secretary of State of the United States and the members of the United States Congress Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 05 Feb 2026

  • Burkina Faso: Türk calls on authorities to halt rapid closure of civic space OHCHR 05 Feb 2026 -- UN Human Rights Chief Volker Türk on Thursday called on the Government of Burkina Faso to put an end to all forms of repression of civic space, and to overturn its intention to prohibit all political parties.

  • 'Human rights cannot wait': Türk launches $400 million appeal for 2026 UN News 05 Feb 2026 -- UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk launched a $400 million appeal on Thursday in Geneva, as his office (OHCHR) continues to operate "in survival mode".
  • UN Human Rights Office launches USD 400 million appeal to address global human rights needs OHCHR 05 Feb 2026 -- UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Volker Türk on Thursday launched a USD 400 million funding appeal for 2026 to address global human rights needs, warning that with mounting crises, the world cannot afford a human rights system in crisis.

  • World News in Brief: UN condemns attack on civilians in Nigeria, updates from Gaza and West Bank, relief flights to resume in Yemen UN News 05 Feb 2026 -- The UN Secretary-General on Thursday strongly condemned a deadly terrorist attack in Kwara state in western Nigeria, where more than 100 people were reportedly killed.
